jokes.text: Popcorn? Really? Cool. Yeah, the hell with Atkins, huh? Oh. Yeah. Oh, why blow it now? I'll fry up some bacon.
jokes.setup: Father offers popcorn despite Atkins diet awareness
jokes.punchline: Doubles down with bacon when caught in contradiction
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation showing Michael's self-aware stubbornness. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Bees? Beads. Beads?
jokes.setup: Gob announces he got a job with 'beads'
jokes.punchline: Michael mishears as 'bees' repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Classic Arrested Development wordplay that becomes a running gag. Perfect economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Gob's not on board.
jokes.setup: Discussion about beads business
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'on board' when Gob thinks it's about bees
jokes.explanation: Clever pun building on the bees mishearing. Shows AD's layered writing style.

jokes.text: Z-z-z-z-z! Bz-z-z-z-z-z! We'll see who brings in more honey.
jokes.setup: Gob decides to start his own business after rejection
jokes.punchline: Makes bee sounds and commits to the bee misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - commits fully to his misunderstanding with characteristic absurdity.

jokes.text: He's thinking about bees again.
jokes.setup: Gob just made bee sounds and stormed off
jokes.punchline: Michael's understated reaction to the obvious
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael deadpan delivery. Perfect straight-man reaction.

jokes.text: Hey, you put an ugly kid in, you can't be surprised when an ugly adult comes out.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains Lindsay's failed photo-aging business
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's brutally honest assessment of her service failure
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures Lindsay's shallow, brutal honesty about her failed business.

jokes.text: Great. Before you know it, I'll be in the red. And you can take that to the bank.
jokes.setup: Lindsay agrees to work for money
jokes.punchline: Uses 'in the red' (losing money) as if it's positive
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's financial ignorance through malapropism. Character-consistent humor.

jokes.text: Who'd want a bee as a gift?
jokes.setup: Discussion about making money from Gob's 'bee' business
jokes.punchline: Michael continues treating the bee misunderstanding as real
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag with Michael's logical but absurd question about bee gifts.

jokes.text: Mother said no carbohydrate for you if you ever gonna get girlfriend that's not old lady.
jokes.setup: Annyong denies Buster food
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest about Buster's dating life with older women
jokes.explanation: Annyong's blunt delivery of uncomfortable truth about Buster's pattern.

jokes.text: Good to see you. You know what? Don't get too close to me. 'Cause I've got an itch you can't believe. I think something laid eggs on me.
jokes.setup: George Sr. greets his lawyer warmly
jokes.punchline: Immediately shares disgusting personal information
jokes.explanation: Classic George Sr. oversharing. Good setup/punchline structure with cringe factor.

jokes.text: And now I'm expected to climb back on top of Kitty and do my thing again.
jokes.setup: Discussion about dealing with Kitty's demands
jokes.punchline: Gob interprets this as a sexual assignment
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob misunderstanding with sexual undertones. Character-consistent but predictable.

jokes.text: He's got bees! No bees! Get it!
jokes.setup: Gob mentions his 'babies' (bees)
jokes.punchline: Prison chaos as guards think he actually has bees
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the running gag - the misunderstanding causes real chaos.

jokes.text: You know, thanks to this diet craze, the last time we had a customer, the banana stand was still green.
jokes.setup: George Michael talks about the low-carb diet affecting business
jokes.punchline: Metaphor about the stand being 'green' (new) when they last had customers
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with 'green' meaning both unripe and new. Well-crafted joke for George Michael.

jokes.text: Lose it. No, lose the whole kid. We'll just go with the Iraq piece.
jokes.setup: George Michael delivers his banana stand joke for TV
jokes.punchline: Producer immediately rejects both joke and George Michael entirely
jokes.explanation: Brutally efficient rejection that escalates from joke to person. Dark but perfectly economical.

jokes.text: Can we please have one conversation that's not about my rack, Michael?
jokes.setup: Michael makes reference to seeing Kitty's breasts again
jokes.punchline: Kitty's exasperated but matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor showing Kitty's frustration with the running gag about her.

jokes.text: Well, I want a hamburger and French fries, but I can't have the bun or potatoes. Get real.
jokes.setup: Kitty demands control of the Bluth Company
jokes.punchline: Michael compares it to wanting impossible diet foods
jokes.explanation: Smart analogy tying current diet obsession to impossible business demand. Well-crafted.

jokes.text: The book, initially unsuccessful, had suddenly caught fire, although, strangely, only in the gay community.
jokes.setup: Tobias finds his self-help book in a store
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals it's popular with gay men (Tobias being obliviously gay-coded)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant layered joke that works on multiple levels. Perfect character integration with ongoing subplot.

jokes.text: Well, that would've worked if you'd sprung for a bigger sink.
jokes.setup: Reference to Lindsay's failed pet-coloring business
jokes.punchline: Blames the failure on equipment rather than concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay deflection - never admits her ideas are bad, just execution.

jokes.text: Some girl. I mean, she barely has a face. You couldn't pick her out of a lineup of one.
jokes.setup: Lindsay describes George Michael's date dismissively
jokes.punchline: Escalates to impossible scenario of being unidentifiable alone
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from petty insult to absurd impossibility. Classic mean Lindsay.

jokes.text: Now, a lot of this damage is just due to shoddy workmanship, but the real surprise here is that these homes appear to be American-built to begin with.
jokes.setup: Reporter discusses damaged Iraqi palaces
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're American-built, implicating the Bluths
jokes.explanation: Important plot revelation delivered as news report. Sets up major story implications.

jokes.text: Does that look a little like our kitchen island?
jokes.setup: Family watches news about Iraqi palaces
jokes.punchline: Lindsay notices their own design elements in the footage
jokes.explanation: Perfect oblivious observation that confirms the family's involvement. Visual comedy.

jokes.text: There's a good chance I may have committed some light treason. This might even be considered light treason.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes his father's crimes are serious
jokes.punchline: George Sr. and narrator both downplay treason as 'light'
jokes.explanation: Iconic AD phrase - treating treason casually. Perfect character voice and became cultural reference.

jokes.text: What about macaroni... let me finish... salad?
jokes.setup: Gob asks about diet-friendly foods
jokes.punchline: Tries to make macaroni diet-friendly by adding 'salad'
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's flawed logic about diet loopholes. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: This is a side of you I'd never noticed before. Have they always been that big?
jokes.setup: Gob seems touched by Kitty's encouragement
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just staring at her breasts
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection showing Gob's shallow nature. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: He never told me where he was going, but he used to come back from those business trips reeking of lamb. And people would send figs... not even at Christmas. In the middle of October. Oh, and once, someone sent him a toe, and he went white as a sheet.
jokes.setup: Lucille lists evidence of George's Middle East connections
jokes.punchline: Escalates from food smells to receiving a human toe
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane details to shocking revelation. Classic AD absurdity.

jokes.text: Zero hour, Michael. It's the end of the line. I'm the first born. Sick of playing second fiddle. Always third in line for everything. Tired of finishing fourth. Being the fifth wheel. There are six things I'm mad about, and I'm taking over.
jokes.setup: Gob declares he's taking charge
jokes.punchline: Lists grievances in ascending numerical order
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating structure with numerical progression. Shows Gob's dramatic but confused nature.

jokes.text: I've made a huge mistake.
jokes.setup: Gob realizes taking over means dealing with family problems
jokes.punchline: Delivers his signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect deployment of iconic catchphrase. Maximum economy and character integration.

jokes.text: The Man Inside Me. For Lindsay, my rock. I could not have done this without him.
jokes.setup: Tobias reads dedication in his book
jokes.punchline: Uses masculine pronouns for Lindsay due to his pronoun change rule
jokes.explanation: Layered joke combining book title innuendo with oblivious pronoun usage.

jokes.text: For there is a man inside me, and only when he's finally out can I walk free of pain.
jokes.setup: Tobias reads from his self-help book
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about coming out while being completely oblivious
jokes.explanation: Perfect double meaning - works as both self-help and coming out metaphor. Brilliant writing.

jokes.text: I was hoping for more women. Perhaps I shouldn't have just used 'he.' I think that was a mistake.
jokes.setup: Tobias notices his audience is all men
jokes.punchline: Blames pronoun choice rather than realizing the obvious reason
jokes.explanation: Shows Tobias's complete lack of self-awareness about why gay men attend his readings.

jokes.text: First time. I've never heard of a second.
jokes.setup: Michael asks how they punish treason
jokes.punchline: George Sr. matter-of-factly notes you don't survive the first execution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with George Sr.'s characteristic casual tone about serious matters.

jokes.text: - First time. - I've never heard of a second.
jokes.setup: Discussion of treason punishment
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry observation about the finality of execution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan timing about the ultimate consequences.

jokes.text: Are you kidding? I don't need to brag that some people haven't even been on a date!
jokes.setup: Maeby asks if George Michael told anyone about their 'date'
jokes.punchline: Insults George Michael while trying to keep their relationship secret
jokes.explanation: Shows Maeby's mean streak while highlighting the uncomfortable cousin dynamic.

jokes.text: Your mom had a good feeling about him.
jokes.setup: Michael asks why George Sr. didn't realize Saddam wouldn't pay
jokes.punchline: Reveals Lucille endorsed the business relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - George Sr. deflecting blame while revealing Lucille's involvement.

jokes.text: I'm gonna get a lethal injection because my son won't eat a potato.
jokes.setup: Michael storms out after their argument
jokes.punchline: George Sr. connects his potential execution to Michael's diet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd connection between high stakes and trivial diet issues. Perfect AD logic.

jokes.text: You can shop... I love my... Can't breathe. I can't breathe!
jokes.setup: George Sr. giving passionate polygraph testimony
jokes.punchline: Apparent heart attack interrupts his patriotic speech
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy timing with dramatic irony since we later learn it's fake.

jokes.text: What kind of diet is this? It's too much meat. I want all of you off this immediately. Except you.
jokes.setup: Lucille blames George Sr.'s heart attack on the Atkins diet
jokes.punchline: Excludes Buster from going off the diet
jokes.explanation: Shows Lucille's controlling nature and favoritism while maintaining diet theme.

jokes.text: Also, Dale, one of the gentlemen from my reading, is a nurse here, so we were able to park right out front.
jokes.setup: Lindsay appreciates Tobias coming to the hospital
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had help from a male nurse he met at his book reading
jokes.explanation: Continues the running theme while showing Tobias's obliviousness to the implications.

jokes.text: I did. I have poison oak. Do you believe it? Where the hell did I get that?
jokes.setup: Barry being asked if he talked to a doctor about George
jokes.punchline: Barry only caring about his own minor medical issue during a family crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment showing his complete self-absorption and incompetence as a lawyer.

jokes.text: I'd rather not, obviously. Don't know what I'd say. Why do I have to be the one? I don't need this. Why does this have to become my problem? No. I'm out. Forget it. Find somebody else. I'm sick of it. I'm sick of doing everything for this family.
jokes.setup: Michael reluctantly agrees to speak for the family
jokes.punchline: Escalates into full rant about always being responsible
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael's perpetual frustration with family responsibility.

jokes.text: I wanted to watch Hindsight, and you weren't there. I want to pack up and desert the family, and you don't want to. Is it this new girlfriend?
jokes.setup: Michael frustrated with George Michael's changing behavior
jokes.punchline: Lists examples of George Michael not supporting his plans
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's frustration when his usually compliant son develops independence.

jokes.text: The state or the police. Maybe the Magicians' Alliance will pick up some slack.
jokes.setup: Michael doesn't know who would care for the family if they left
jokes.punchline: Suggests the Magicians' Alliance as a caregiving option
jokes.explanation: Absurd suggestion that references ongoing Gob subplot. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: Ever since he's been dating this Annie McNo-face, he's been blowing me off.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about George Michael's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Adopts Lindsay's cruel nickname for the girl
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's petty side by adopting his sister's mean nickname.

jokes.text: It's just glucose. We're all trying to stay away from sugar.
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to remove his IV
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes it conflicts with their diet obsession
jokes.explanation: Ties medical situation back to running diet theme. Shows priorities are skewed.

jokes.text: Old bear! He likes the honey!
jokes.setup: Gob calls his father 'old bear'
jokes.punchline: Connects it to honey and his bee business obsession
jokes.explanation: Continues bee theme while creating new family nickname. Shows Gob's obsession persisting.

jokes.text: Heart attack never stop old Big Bear. I didn't even know we were calling him Big Bear. We never had a chance to!
jokes.setup: Annyong uses the 'Big Bear' nickname
jokes.punchline: Michael points out they just invented the nickname
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about nickname creation. Good self-awareness of the writing process.

jokes.text: We lost him. He just, uh, got away from us. I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Family expects news about George Sr.'s death
jokes.punchline: Doctor reveals he escaped rather than died
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that recontextualizes the entire episode ending. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: Why would a doctor say he's 'gone' when he means he's escaped? God, I've missed bread. Mmm. Wouldn't you say, 'He left out the window' or, 'The room's empty.'
jokes.setup: Family processes the news of George Sr.'s escape
jokes.punchline: Lindsay critiques doctor's word choice while eating bread
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's priorities - more interested in bread than father's escape. Character-driven.

jokes.text: Maybe we should kiss again and teach them a lesson about obvious.
jokes.setup: Maeby and George Michael discuss family being oblivious
jokes.punchline: Suggests more cousin kissing to teach about obviousness
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor about the inappropriate cousin dynamic. Sets up future storylines.
